title: Analytical Methodology for the Sign Study of Chalcographic Prints
author: Masci, Marco Valerio
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The research proposes an analytical protocol for the comparative sign study of chalcographic prints. The protocol was developed on Rembrandt's "Christ Heals the Sick" print (also called the Hundred Florins Print). The study proposes an investigation technique that uses various kind of digital tools (software and hardware, "graphic", "photographic" and "audio"), extrapolating the parametric information that can be derived from vector and raster data. This made it possible to correlate different images (relating to different states of the same work) to verify the correspondence of the signs and other indicators useful for comparative evaluation. The investigation constitutes an analytical protocol for the historical/artistic evaluation of the chalcographic work. From page 197 the Italian version of the paper.
